sourceMovie.title: True Romance
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit True Romance for the same reason it broke through in 1993: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tony Scott keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Scarface
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If True Romance clicked for you, Scarface (1983) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys for Life
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If True Romance clicked for you, Bad Boys for Life (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If True Romance clicked for you, Bad Boys II (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after True Romance?
faq.answer: Try Scarface, Bad Boys for Life, Bad Boys II—each shares DNA with True Romance while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is True Romance?
faq.answer: True Romance (1993) is best described as Action, Crime, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is True Romance worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
